content.text2: An AI operations layer that sits in front of your CRM
content.text3: Nortra is an AI response and operations platform that intercepts pre-deal CRM workflows — acting as the system of action before a lead ever reaches close. I led front-end development at Samarix, building operational dashboards that surface AI-generated responses and enable teams to manage, action, and route leads intelligently. Built with Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion.

content.text2: CRMs capture deals. They don't close them.
content.text3: Sales teams spend a disproportionate amount of time on pre-deal work — triaging leads, crafting initial responses, qualifying intent — before a deal even enters the CRM pipeline. Nortra exists to automate and accelerate that layer. The front-end challenge was making complex, AI-driven data flows feel fast and intuitive for non-technical sales operators.

content.caption: The operations dashboard — AI-generated responses surfaced and ready to action

content.text3: The key design and development challenge was avoiding cognitive overload. Sales operators need to triage dozens of AI-processed leads quickly. I built the interface around a clear action-first hierarchy — each lead card surfaces the AI response, the recommended action, and the one-click CTA without burying them in metadata. Data visualisation components helped team leads track response quality and pipeline velocity at a glance.

content.caption: Lead cards designed for fast triage — action in one click
Pipeline velocity and AI response quality tracked in real time

content.text3: I implemented seamless, animated UI patterns using Framer Motion throughout the platform. When complex data flows are in motion — leads being processed, responses being generated, statuses updating — animation is what makes the system feel alive rather than broken. Every transition was designed to carry the user's attention forward, not interrupt it.

content.caption: Animated state transitions during AI processing keep operators informed without blocking them
Response management interface — approve, edit, or re-generate in context

content.text3: In an AI operations tool, the front-end performance IS the product. A laggy interface breaks the trust operators place in AI-generated recommendations. I applied code splitting, lazy loading, and image optimisation throughout — keeping Lighthouse scores high and ensuring the platform felt as fast as the AI pipeline behind it.
